title: Variable tissue collection and processing cassette
abstract: An apparatus includes a tissue processing cassette and a biopsy device. The tissue processing cassette is sized to be used in a pathology laboratory for purposes of dehydrating, embedding and sectioning. The tissue processing cassette has at least one distal opening through which tissue samples are received and having a floor with a plurality of openings. The biopsy device is for cutting tissue samples and has a cassette holder configured to translate relative to a proximal end of the biopsy device. The cassette holder is adapted to receive the tissue processing cassette. The cassette holder has a fluid path adapted to be coupled to a vacuum source and be in fluid communication with the interior of the tissue processing cassette through the openings of the floor such that the vacuum from the vacuum source facilitates the transfer of the cut tissue samples into the received tissue processing cassette.
claims:
Claims We Claim: 1. A sample collection and processing apparatus comprising: a tissue processing cassette sized to be used in a pathology laboratory for purposes of dehydrating, embedding and sectioning, the tissue processing cassette having at least one distal opening through which tissue samples are received and having a floor with a plurality of openings; and a biopsy device for cutting tissue samples and having a cassette holder configured to translate relative to a proximal end of the biopsy device and adapted to receive the tissue processing cassette, the cassette holder having a fluid path adapted to be coupled to a vacuum source and in fluid communication with the interior of the tissue processing cassette through the openings of the floor such that the vacuum from the vacuum source facilitates the transfer of the cut tissue samples into the received tissue processing cassette. 2. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the tissue processing cassette includes a plurality of distal openings. 3. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the tissue processing cassette includes a plurality of distal openings, wherein the cassette holder is translatable relative to a cutter of the biopsy device to index each distal opening with the cutter. 4. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the plurality of openings of the tissue processing cassette are defined by a plurality of square-shaped openings in the floor of the tissue processing cassette. 5. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the cassette holder defines a plurality of sample openings, wherein the tissue processing cassette defines a plurality of distal openings with each distal opening corresponding to a sample opening of the cassette holder, wherein the cassette holder is translatable relative to a cutter of the biopsy device to successively index each sample opening with the cutter. 6. The apparatus of claim 5, wherein each sample opening of the cassette holder is adjacent to another sample opening such that the plurality of sample openings are together arranged along a common axis. 7. The apparatus of claim 5, wherein the cassette holder further includes a plurality of vacuum openings, wherein each vacuum opening is proximate to and corresponds to a sample opening. 8. The apparatus of claim 7, wherein the cassette holder further includes a plurality of vacuum channels configured to direct vacuum from each vacuum opening and through the floor of the tissue processing cassette. 9. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the cassette holder defines an angled portion, wherein the angled portion is configured to deflect one or more tissue samples into the tissue processing cassette when the tissue processing cassette is disposed in the cassette holder. 10. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the tissue processing cassette includes an insert removably received within the interior of the tissue processing cassette, wherein the insert is configured to define a plurality of tissue sample chambers within the tissue processing cassette when the insert is received therein. 11. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the tissue processing cassette includes an angled identification area configured to receive identifying information thereon. 12. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the tissue processing cassette includes a lid connected to a base of the tissue processing cassette with a hinge. 13. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the tissue processing cassette includes a plurality of interior walls, wherein the interior walls define a plurality of tissue sample chambers with each tissue sample chamber configured to receive a tissue sample. 14. The apparatus of claim 1, further comprising a coupler configured to couple the cassette holder to a proximal end of the biopsy device, wherein the coupler includes a track configured to slidably receive the cassette holder. 15. The apparatus of claim 14, wherein the coupler further includes an indexer configured to bias the cassette holder towards a plurality of discrete translational positions relative to the coupler. 16. A sample collection and processing apparatus for use with a biopsy device, wherein the system comprises: a tissue sample holder assembly including a slidable member defining a mount, wherein the mount includes a cassette chamber and a vacuum chamber oriented below the cassette chamber; and a tissue processing cassette sized to be used in a pathology laboratory for purposes of dehydrating, embedding and sectioning, the tissue processing cassette having a plurality of tissue sample chambers, wherein the mount is configured to receive the tissue processing cassette within the cassette chamber such that tissue samples are received from the biopsy device in different tissue sample chambers as the slidable member translates under vacuum supplied to the vacuum chamber. 17. The apparatus of claim 16, wherein the vacuum chamber of the mount includes a plurality of walls configured to direct vacuum into a particular tissue sample chamber of the tissue processing cassette. 18. The apparatus of claim 17, wherein the tissue processing cassette includes a plurality of internal walls defining each tissue sample chamber of the plurality of tissue sample 

 chambers, wherein each internal wall of the tissue processing cassette corresponds to each wall of the mount. 19. The apparatus of claim 18, wherein the tissue sample holder defines a plurality of sample openings and a plurality of vacuum openings, wherein each vacuum opening is disposed between a wall of the mount, wherein each sample opening is in communication with each tissue sample chamber of the tissue processing cassette when the tissue processing cassette is received within the mount. 20. A method of collecting samples for analysis by a pathology laboratory, the method comprising: attaching to a biopsy device a tissue processing cassette sized to be used in a pathology laboratory for purposes of dehydrating, embedding, and sectioning; cutting a tissue sample with the tissue processing cassette attached to the biopsy device so as to receive the cut tissue sample into the attached tissue processing cassette under the direction of vacuum applied to a bottom surface of the tissue processing cassette; translating the cassette relative to a cutter of the biopsy device to receive another cut tissue sample into the attached tissue processing cassette; detaching the tissue processing cassette from the biopsy device; and inserting the tissue processing cassette with the cut tissue sample into a container containing a fixing agent.
